instruction:
Given that the function $f ( x )$ is continuous on $(-\infty,+\infty)$, and $f ( x )=( x+1 )^{2}+2 {\int_{0}^{x}} f ( t ) \mathrm{d} t$, then for $n \geqslant2$ ,$f^{( n )} \left( 0 \right)=$ ( ).

Options:
A. $5 \cdot2^{n-1}$
B. $6 \cdot2^{n-1}$
C. $3 \cdot2^{n-2}$
D. $4 \cdot2^{n+1}$
E. $4 \cdot2^{n+2}$
F. $7 \cdot2^{n+1}$
G. $3 \cdot2^{n-3}$
H. $3 \cdot2^{n+1}$
I. $8 \cdot2^{n-1}$
J. $10 \cdot2^{n-1}$
